goal: Evaluate whether installed Unreal Water/WaterAdvanced plugin materials can improve the generated downstream river-eye preview captures before custom production water shaders are built.

test_run.build: SmokeEmIfYouGotEmEditor Mac Development succeeded with UE_5.8

observed_failure.summary: The engine WaterAdvanced material candidate produced a near-black river surface on the generated procedural river ribbon, making the downstream river-eye captures worse than the active vertex-color water preview.
likely_causes: plugin water material expects WaterBody or WaterZone data not present on the generated ProceduralMeshComponent ribbon
default plugin material parameters are not tuned for the custom preview mesh, manual exposure, or source-mask-driven river colors
the generated mesh currently needs a purpose-built material or Water plugin integration rather than a direct material swap
blocked_use: do not use the tested engine water material candidate as the active generated preview water path
do not claim Water plugin integration is validated for the preview river ribbon
do not overwrite the currently readable vertex-color water captures with the failed black-water captures

active_decision.decision: Keep the first-party lit vertex-color water preview material active until a custom water shader or true WaterBody integration is implemented and visually verified.
active_decision.promotion_gate: Any future Water plugin material attempt must be captured for all three rivers and visually checked for nonblack water, readable waves/foam, hazard visibility, and no masking of swimmer/rescue targets before it replaces the active preview material.

next_actions: Prototype a purpose-built preview water material with normal/foam texture inputs rather than direct Water plugin material reuse.
Evaluate true WaterBodyRiver or WaterMesh integration only after terrain, channel, and spline geometry are production-shaped.
Keep regenerated captures honest: mark current water as readable proxy material, not production photoreal water.
